title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: July 3, 2026: Offseason Heats Up with Blockbuster Trades and Summer League Kicks Off!

items.headline: Blockbuster! Jaylen Brown Traded to 76ers, Paul George Heads to Celtics
items.summary: In a seismic offseason move, the Boston Celtics have traded five-time All-Star Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers in exchange for Paul George and multiple future draft picks. Brown, the 2024 NBA Finals MVP, was the league's fourth-leading scorer last season. This trade comes as a major surprise, transforming both Eastern Conference contenders.

items.headline: Knicks Join the Race for LeBron James, Multiple Landing Spots in Play
items.summary: After informing the Lakers he wouldn't return for a ninth season, LeBron James' free agency saga continues with the New York Knicks reportedly showing interest. The four-time MVP has multiple preferred destinations in play.

items.headline: No NBA Regular Season Games Scheduled
items.summary: The NBA regular season is currently in its offseason. No games were played on July 2, 2026.

items.headline: Deni Avdija: Blazers' Cornerstone in Contract Talks
items.summary: Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija, a recent first-time All-Star and top-three finalist for Most Improved Player, is a key focus for Portland this offseason. While he has two years remaining on his current deal, he becomes eligible for an extension starting October 21, 2026, with the Blazers expected to prioritize a long-term deal.

items.headline: NBA to Test 'One Free Throw' Rule and Sensor Balls in Summer League
items.summary: The NBA is introducing two significant rule changes for testing in the upcoming Summer League: a 'one free throw' rule for all fouls (except in the last two minutes of the fourth quarter and overtime) and the use of sensor-equipped basketballs for data collection. The California Classic Summer League begins tonight.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Traded to Miami Heat
items.summary: In another major offseason development, the Milwaukee Bucks have traded two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat. Miami landed the 'biggest prize in NBA free agency' with this move.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard Dealt to Raptors in Clippers Trade
items.summary: The Los Angeles Clippers have traded Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors in exchange for Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick. This move marks a significant shift for both franchises.

items.headline: Tari Eason Agrees to $81.5M Extension, Reportedly Unhappy
items.summary: Rockets forward Tari Eason agreed to a five-year, $81.5 million contract extension, though reports suggest he wasn't entirely thrilled with the deal.

items.headline: Anfernee Simons Signs Two-Year Deal with 76ers
items.summary: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million contract with the Philadelphia 76ers, adding depth to their backcourt.

items.headline: Fred VanVleet's Injury Reportedly More Severe Than Expected
items.summary: Rockets guard Fred VanVleet revealed his knee injury involved a torn ACL and both menisci, an injury more severe than initially disclosed. This has significantly impacted Houston's offseason plans.

items.headline: California Classic Summer League Tips Off Tonight!
items.summary: The NBA Summer League officially begins tonight with the California Classic in San Francisco. Rookies and prospects will showcase their talents.
